title: Optimized Code Output vs Fast Compilation
problem_statement:
A game engine's custom shader compiler must produce highly optimized GPU code (within 5% of hand-tuned assembly performance) while supporting the rapid iteration cycles that game developers demand.

The current -O3 optimization pipeline applies 14 optimization passes including loop unrolling, instruction scheduling, register allocation with graph coloring, dead code elimination, and cross-function inlining. This produces code that runs within 2% of hand-tuned performance but takes 47 seconds per shader compilation. A full scene rebuild (2,400 shaders) takes 31 hours.

Reducing to -O1 (4 passes) compiles each shader in 0.8 seconds (full rebuild in 32 minutes) but produces code 34% slower at runtime, causing frame rate drops below the 60fps minimum for VR content.

The compiler must produce maximally optimized code while completing compilation fast enough for interactive iteration.

ground_truth.ifr_baseline: The compiler produces perfectly optimized code with zero additional compilation time
